Patch Set 2:

Is there a way we can create the symlinks at install, instead of adding them to source control?

Patch Set 2:

They probably could be created at install, but it's less straightforward than checking in the symlinks directly.  ZFS checks in symlinks directly for their scripts.  For example:

### zfs/cmd/zed/zed.d/ ###
pool_import-led.sh -> statechange-led.sh
vdev_attach-led.sh -> statechange-led.sh
vdev_clear-led.sh -> statechange-led.sh
Patch Set 2: Code-Review+1
Patch Set 2: Code-Review+2
Change has been successfully cherry-picked as e11a47da71a2e2482e4c4cf582d663cd76a2ecab by Oleg Drokin

parents.subject: LU-14663 mdc: start changelog thread upon first access

commit.subject: LU-12836 osd-zfs: Catch all ZFS pool change events
commit.message:
LU-12836 osd-zfs: Catch all ZFS pool change events

This change adds the following symlinks:

  vdev_attach-lustre -> statechange-lustre.sh
  vdev_remove-lustre -> statechange-lustre.sh
  vdev_clear-lustre -> statechange-lustre.sh

This makes it so the statechange-lustre.sh script is also called on
all ZFS events that could change the pool state.

Signed-off-by: Tony Hutter <hutter2@llnl.gov>
Change-Id: I18edc86749e8ab91bb45f21aafd3fd47e78cbaef
Reviewed-on: https://review.whamcloud.com/43552
Reviewed-by: Olaf Faaland-LLNL <faaland1@llnl.gov>
Tested-by: jenkins <devops@whamcloud.com>
Tested-by: Maloo <maloo@whamcloud.com>
Reviewed-by: Nathaniel Clark <nclark@whamcloud.com>
Reviewed-by: Oleg Drokin <green@whamcloud.com>
